Targeted Help

Partial Wedding Planning in Baltimore, Maryland

You've done a lot of the work. Now bring in professional support for the parts that still feel unsettled.

Vendor negotiations, contract review, timeline creation, the final-month stretch before your Maryland wedding: John handles whichever of these you need, without requiring you to hand over the whole project.

Book a Free Consultation View Pricing

What Does Partial Wedding Planning Include?

Partial planning gives you a professional partner for the specific parts where you're stuck, without paying for full-service management of things you've already handled. You're not handing over the whole project. You're getting expert help where it actually matters.

You stay in control of your wedding. John's providing expertise on vendor sourcing and negotiation, contract protection, timeline building, and day-of management. It's the same package whether you need help with one vendor category or five.

Day-of coordination is always included. Every partial planning client gets John managing the full wedding day from setup through send-off, regardless of what else they've used the package for. You won't be on your own when the day arrives.

Book a Free Consultation

Everything Included

  • Up to 10 planning consultations (video, phone, or in-person)
  • Vendor sourcing for up to 5 categories you choose
  • Contract review for each vendor before you sign
  • Full budget creation and management
  • Detailed 15-minute interval timeline
  • Final vendor confirmation calls (2 weeks out)
  • Ceremony rehearsal direction (1 hour)
  • Full wedding day coordination from setup through send-off
  • Emergency kit on hand all day

$800 to $1,800

Depends on vendor categories and planning timeline

Who Is Partial Wedding Planning Right For?

Partial planning works best for couples who've started but hit a specific wall. If you're not sure whether your situation fits, the free consultation will sort it out quickly.

You've booked your venue and photographer but you're stuck on vendors

John sources, vets, and negotiates with the specific vendors you still need. He knows which caterers, florists, and DJs are worth the cost at different price points across Maryland, and he'll tell you honestly which choices make a real difference at your budget level.

You need contracts reviewed before you sign anything else

Wedding vendor contracts have clauses most couples miss until it's too late: catering exclusivity requirements, overtime fees, cancellation terms, and tipping policies that aren't disclosed upfront. John reviews every contract and flags anything that could cost you money.

You've planned everything but the last 6 weeks feel overwhelming

Partial planning can start as close as 4 to 6 weeks before the wedding for final-stretch support: vendor confirmation calls, timeline building, rehearsal coordination, and full day-of management. It's where partial planning earns its cost the most.

You're not sure how to build a wedding timeline

A 15-minute interval timeline is one of the most valuable things a planner provides. It tells every vendor exactly where to be and when, which prevents the communication gaps that cause delays and stress on the day. John builds these for all partial planning clients.

How Does Partial Planning Compare to Full-Service and Day-of?

Partial planning is the middle option. Here's an honest breakdown so you can figure out which level fits where you are right now.

Day-of Only Partial Planning Full-Service
Start time 2-3 weeks out 4-8 months out At engagement
Vendor sourcing None (you booked all) Up to 5 categories All vendors
Contract review Not included Included Included
Budget management Not included Included Included
Day-of coverage Yes (this is the service) Included Included
Maryland cost $500 to $1,500 $800 to $1,800 $1,500 to $2,800
Best for You've planned it all You've started but need targeted help You haven't started yet

Partial Planning: Common Questions

What does partial wedding planning include?

Up to 10 planning consultations, vendor sourcing for up to 5 categories you choose, contract review before you sign, budget management, a detailed timeline, final vendor confirmation calls, rehearsal direction, and full day-of coordination. Day-of coverage is included in every partial planning package.

How much does partial planning cost in Maryland?

Partial planning with John runs $800 to $1,800 depending on how many vendor categories are involved and how much work has already been done. See the full pricing comparison.

What's the difference between partial planning and full-service?

Full-service starts at engagement and manages everything. Partial planning joins mid-process and focuses on specific areas where you need help. If you've already booked your venue and photographer and need help with the rest of the vendors plus day-of management, partial planning is usually the right call.

When should I book partial planning?

Ideally 4 to 8 months before your wedding. That gives enough time to source missing vendors, review contracts, build a proper timeline, and get organized before the final stretch. If you're closer than 4 months out, reach out anyway and John can tell you what's still realistic to accomplish.

Can I add partial planning if I've already booked day-of coordination?

Yes. If your planning situation changes after booking and you realize you need more help than day-of coordination provides, John can discuss upgrading to a partial planning package. The day-of coordination fee already paid would apply toward the partial planning cost.

Just Need Day-of Help?

Day-of coordination manages your wedding day without the prior planning involvement. Starts at $500.
